RULING:
DEFENDANT MOORE DRY DOCK COMPANY ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS MOORE SECURITIES COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Moore Dry Dock Company erroneously sued as Moore securities Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Johnson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether the insulation aboard the USS CARTER HALL to which Mr. Johnson was exposed was original to the ship's construction by defendant. Order after hearing and order re: defendant's objections to plaintiffs' evidence offered in opposition to defendant's motion for summary judgment / adjudication were signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

E. Jacob Lubarsky of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4000), appearing for defendant / opposing party Metalclad Insulation Corporation

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is August 10, 2015 at 11:15am in Department 503. The 120th day is August 14, 2015. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 30, 2015. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless plaintiffs stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Before the Court will hear any discovery motion the parties involved in the discovery dispute must schedule and attend an informal discovery conference with the Court. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than May 29, 2015. Responses to the demand are due no later than June 5, 2015. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by August 3, 2015 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on August 6, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is July 24, 2015. Expert discovery cut-off date is August 7, 2015. No later than April 30, 2015 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than April 30, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on June 10, 2015 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on August 10, 2015, or soon thereafter, at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by August 6, 2015 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kimberly L. Avery of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5074 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth N. Branham of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP (415-4386682), for defendant / moving party Moore Dry Dock Company (erroneously sued as Moore Securities Co.)
Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Richard Johnson and Marcella Johnson
Mishan Wroe of SCHIFF HARDIN LLP (415-901-8724), appearing for defendant / joinder Owens-Illinois, Inc.

RULING:
DEFENDANT MOORE DRY DOCK COMPANY'S MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant Moore Dry Dock Company's motion to dismiss the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied without prejudice. Defendant failed to demonstrate that North Dakota is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) Defendant's alternate motion to stay the action is denied without prejudice. The balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation remain in California. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri l. Jackson, Reporter: Kimberly L. Avery of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5074 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
